title: Fragrant Silky Italian Veal Ossobuco
description: A one-pot Italian dish featuring veal ossobuco, flavored with herbs and finished with a silky texture, suitable for a Buddhist temple gathering.
instructions: Season the veal shanks with salt and let them sit at room temperature for 30 minutes before cooking.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Sear the veal shanks until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the veal shanks from the pot and set them aside.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery to the pot and cook until they are tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the white wine to the pot and bring it to a boil, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Add the beef broth, thyme, and bay leaves to the pot and bring it to a boil.
Return the veal shanks to the pot and make sure they are covered with the cooking liquid.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the oven, where it will cook at 300 degrees Fahrenheit for 2 1/2 hours, or until the veal is tender and the meat is easily shredded with a fork.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Strain the cooking liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, discarding the solids.
Whisk in the unflavored gelatin and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the gelatin is dissolved and the sauce is silky and smooth.
